perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement will begin on the date of this Agreement and will end on October 3, 2005 unless terminated earlier by one party upon giving the other party at least 30 days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement's term begins on the effective date and ends on October 3, 2005. Either Party can terminate with immediate effect upon written notification to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies a 30-day notice period for termination. The change now allows immediate termination upon written notice, contradicting the original 30-day requirement. The term 'term of this Agreement' has been interchanged with 'This Agreement's term'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM. This Agreement's term begins on the effective date and ends on October 3, 2005. Either Party can terminate with immediate effect upon written notification to the other party. 6. Non-Disclosure. Recipient will use best efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the Information both during and after the term of this Agreement and will not disclose the Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the University for a period of three years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination clause from requiring a 30-day written notice to allowing immediate termination upon written notice. The contradicted text repeats this new immediate termination clause. While they are the same now, the contradicted text was taken from elsewhere in the contract and there is an in-text contradiction because the original text specified a 30 day notice while the changed text and contradicted text now allow for immediate termination, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will use best efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the Information both during and after the term of this Agreement and will not disclose the Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the University for a period of three years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party must take reasonable steps to protect the Secrecy but is not obligated to seek prior consent for disclosures to its affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates 'best efforts' to maintain confidentiality and requires prior written consent from the University before disclosing the Information to any third party for three years. The modified version, which replace 'Recipient' with 'Receiving Party', 'Information' with 'Secrecy', eases the standard to 'reasonable steps' and removes the requirement for prior consent when disclosing to affiliates. This creates a contradiction regarding the level of effort required for confidentiality and the restrictions on disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure. The Receiving Party must take reasonable steps to protect the Secrecy but is not obligated to seek prior consent for disclosures to its affiliates. 7. Return or Destruction of Information. At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'best efforts' for confidentiality and prior written consent for disclosure to third parties, including affiliates, for three years. The changed text lowers the standard to 'reasonable steps' and removes the consent requirement for disclosures to affiliates. The contradicted text repeats the 'reasonable steps' standard and removal of consent for affiliates. The contradiction arises because the modified text introduces a different standard of care and disclosure requirements that are incompatible with the initial stricter obligations outlined in the original text and later reiterated in the contradicted section. This misalignment of terminology and obligations creates a strong contradiction regarding confidentiality and disclosure, and the 'misaligned terminology' makes it a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon The University's written request or at the end of this Contract, The Receiver must return all Data, but can keep copies for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the 'Recipient' to return or destroy 'Information' at the University's direction, prohibiting any retention of copies. The modified text, replace 'Recipient' with 'The Receiver', 'Information' with 'Data' allows the 'Receiver' to retain copies for archival purposes, contradicting the original restriction on retaining copies. The contradiction directly relates to whether or not copies of the confidential information can be kept.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction of Information. Upon The University's written request or at the end of this Contract, The Receiver must return all Data, but can keep copies for archival purposes. 8. No Waiver. No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing and signed by the University. A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will not be construed to be a waiver of a subsequent breach of the same provision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a clause regarding the return or destruction of data. Originally, the Recipient had to return or destroy the Information, with no copies retained. The changed text, which is also the contradicted text, allows 'The Receiver' to keep copies for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the original restriction. Since the original text prohibits retention of copies but the changed/contradicted text permits it, this creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not assign all or part of this Agreement without the prior writter consent of the University.
perturbation.changed_text: The Assignee can transfer this contract to its parent Company, irrespective of the University’s Consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text forbids assignment without the University's prior written consent. The modified text now permits assignment to the 'Assignee's' parent company without the University's consent, directly contradicting the original clause. The interchange is on the definition of Recipient with Assignee, and changing the conditions when assigning this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. The Assignee can transfer this contract to its parent Company, irrespective of the University’s Consent. 10. Entire Agreement and Counterpart. This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient cannot assign the agreement without the University's written consent. The changed text allows the Assignee to transfer the contract to its parent company without the University's consent, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text reinforces this contradictory clause, stating that 'The Assignee can transfer this contract to its parent Company, irrespective of the University’s Consent.', thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed under the laws of British Columbia and the applicable laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules. Any action or proceeding brought to enforce the terms of this Agreement will be brought in a court in Vancouver, British Columbia, and the parties hereby consent and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of such court.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract adheres to British Columbia law but disputes will resolve through arbitration in Seattle, Washington.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that disputes will be resolved in a court in Vancouver, British Columbia. The modified text states that disputes will be resolved through arbitration in Seattle, Washington. This directly contradicts the original clause regarding the jurisdiction for dispute resolution. The terminology changed here is the legal process for Governing Law and Juristiction to take place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Contract adheres to British Columbia law but disputes will resolve through arbitration in Seattle, Washington. IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have executed this Agreement on the date first written above. UNIVERSITY OF BRITISH COLUMBIA GENEMAX PHARMACEUTICALS INC. by its duly authorized officer by its duly authorized officer: /s/ David Jones /s/ Ronald Handford David Jones, Associate Director Name : Ronald Handford University-Industry Liaison Office Title: President and CEO
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies dispute resolution in Vancouver, British Columbia, while the changed text mandates arbitration in Seattle, Washington. This creates a direct conflict, as the two clauses specify different locations and methods for resolving disputes. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which shows that the contradiction exists in the document as a whole. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as it changes the legal process for Governing Law and Jurisdiction.
